subject: A European angle on your roof underwriting chart
body:
Hi SF Chronicle team,

(Writing to the SF Chronicle newsroom rather than Megan directly — we couldn't locate a direct contact. Feel free to forward if it's a better fit for them or the desk.)

Really enjoyed your piece compiling the roof underwriting rules across California's top 20 insurers. You did something most coverage skips: you went to the actual guidelines, not the talking points. The chart format makes the carrier consensus impossible to miss.

A quick hello from our side: at My Villa, we bring European construction resilience and the Italian way of living to homes in Los Angeles, designed in exposed reinforced concrete, cemento a vista.

One thought your piece sparked: what California carriers are now treating as a roof red line has been default Mediterranean practice for generations. The cultural lag between the two markets feels like the real story.

Would you be interested in receiving more material on what we are doing, or in setting up a short call with our founder Paolo?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

subject: A European angle on the Washington IBHS program
body:
Hi Insurance News Net team,

(Writing to the Insurance News Net newsroom directly — this is meant for whichever desk is closest to the topic below. Feel free to forward as appropriate.)

Really appreciated the piece on Washington's IBHS Wildfire Prepared Home rollout, particularly the detail on the three-year designation cycle and annual photo verification. That mitigation is treated as an ongoing relationship with the carrier, not a one-time stamp, is the part most coverage misses.

A quick hello from our side: at My Villa, we bring European construction resilience and the Italian way of living to homes in Los Angeles, designed in exposed reinforced concrete, cemento a vista.

One thought your piece sparked: what IBHS is now formalizing as a maintained designation has been default Mediterranean construction practice for generations, built in from day one rather than verified annually. The cultural gap feels like the real story.

Would you be interested in receiving more material on what we are doing, or in setting up a short call with our founder Paolo?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

subject: A different idea of the resilient luxury home for the Westside
body:
Hello,

DIGS readers know the LA Westside and South Bay as well as anyone — which is why I thought of you. As insurability and fire-resilience quietly reshape what a luxury home even means here, the conversation around design is shifting too.

A quick hello from our side: at My Villa, we bring European construction resilience and the Italian way of living to homes in Los Angeles — designed in exposed reinforced concrete, *cemento a vista*. In Europe, building for durability has been the default for generations; we pair it with the Italian sense of light, proportion, and indoor/outdoor flow.

I think there's a feature in how that combination reads on the Westside right now — a home that's both genuinely resilient and beautiful to live in. I'd be glad to offer a first look at a current build, with renders and photos, or a short talk with our founder, Paolo.

Would that be of interest to you?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

subject: A European angle on your wildfire deductibles piece
body:
Hi Washington Post team,

(Writing to the Washington Post newsroom directly — this is meant for whichever desk is closest to the topic below. Feel free to forward as appropriate.)

Really enjoyed your investigation into California's wildfire deductibles. You landed the detail most coverage misses: that a single inquiry was enough to push the Commissioner toward a formal review, which says a lot about how unsettled these policies still are.

A quick hello from our side: at My Villa, we bring European construction resilience and the Italian way of living to homes in Los Angeles, designed in exposed reinforced concrete, cemento a vista.

One thought your piece sparked: the resilience California is now pricing into deductibles has been default Mediterranean building practice for generations. That cultural gap feels like the real story.

Would you be interested in receiving more material on what we are doing, or in setting up a short call with our founder Paolo?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

subject: A European angle on your Client Challenge piece
body:
Hi team,

Your piece on the insurance crisis facing California homeowners hit on something most coverage glosses over — the structural vulnerability that quietly sits at the center of so many denied claims and dropped policies.

My Villa builds homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete, a method rooted in Italian and broader Mediterranean construction tradition, where durability and material honesty have always been inseparable. That approach speaks directly to what your article surfaces: when the building itself is the risk, changing what you build with changes the conversation entirely.

Would you be open to receiving some background materials, or a short call with Paolo Mezzalama, our founder, to explore whether there's an angle worth developing together?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

subject: A European angle on the commissioner candidate statements
body:
Hi Voterguide team,

(Writing to the Voterguide newsroom directly — this is meant for whichever desk is closest to the topic below. Feel free to forward as appropriate.)

The candidate statements make for a revealing read — they show how many competing visions are now circling the same wildfire insurance crisis, from single-payer to market reform, without much consensus on what makes a home insurable.

A quick introduction: My Villa brings European construction resilience and Italian livability to homes in Los Angeles, designed in exposed reinforced concrete, cemento a vista. In Europe, resilience has been default construction practice for generations.

One thought worth following: whichever model wins, the resilience LA is debating has been normal Mediterranean practice for generations. That cultural lag feels like the real story.

Would you be interested in receiving more material on what we are doing, or in setting up a short call with our founder Paolo?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

subject: A European angle on your Who's running for insurance commissioner in CA's 2026 electi piece
body:
Hi team,

Your breakdown of the insurance commissioner candidates — and how each one is framing California's property coverage crisis — was a genuinely useful read. The way you mapped the policy positions against the underlying risk landscape made the stakes very clear.

My Villa builds Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete, a method rooted in Mediterranean construction culture that happens to address many of the resilience concerns driving today's insurance volatility. There is something worth noting in how a centuries-old building tradition quietly speaks to the durability questions California is now legislating around.

Would anyone on the team be open to a brief conversation with our founder, Paolo Mezzalama, or to receiving some background material?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team

subject: A European angle on your LA Project Offers Blueprint for Resilient Housing Amid Wildf piece
body:
Hi team,

Your recent piece on fire-resistant housing in Los Angeles was a thoughtful and well-reported look at what resilient design could mean for communities facing real wildfire risk — the framing around scalable blueprints was particularly compelling.

My Villa is building Italian-designed homes in Los Angeles using reinforced exposed concrete — cemento a vista — a material that has quietly shaped Mediterranean architecture for generations precisely because of its durability under extreme conditions. There is a natural thread connecting the fire-resilient future your article envisions to the way Southern European builders have long approached permanence.

We would love to share more about the project, or arrange a short conversation with our founder Paolo Mezzalama, if that feels useful.

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

subject: One thought on Commissioner Lara's decades-ahead framing
body:
Hi Insurance team,

(Writing to the Insurance newsroom directly — this is meant for whichever desk is closest to the topic below. Feel free to forward as appropriate.)

Really enjoyed your coverage of Commissioner Lara's oversight push, the 'decades ahead, not just today' framing is the part most reporting glosses over, and you gave it room to breathe.

A quick hello from our side: at My Villa, we bring European construction resilience and the Italian way of living to homes in Los Angeles, designed in exposed reinforced concrete, cemento a vista. In Europe, resilience has been default construction practice for generations.

One thought your piece sparked: the long-horizon resilience the Commissioner is asking for has been normal Mediterranean practice since long before insurability became a topic here. That cultural lag feels like the real story.

Would you be interested in receiving more material on what we are doing, or in setting up a short call with our founder Paolo?

Best,
